chief_complaint: A 30-year-old woman presented with shortness of breath and swelling in her legs.
history: She had been using a mercury-containing whitening cream for several months. She had no history of lung or kidney disease, but had been experiencing fatigue and hair loss. She had no known allergies or sensitivities.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80, HR 100, RR 24, O2 sat 92%. Physical examination revealed bilateral lower extremity edema and decreased breath sounds.
labs: Chest enhancement CT: pulmonary embolism. Laboratory tests: nephrotic syndrome, hypothyroidism. Blood and urine were positive for mercury.
